You can execute a debug command by typing it into the field below and pressing [Enter].
Commands aren't case-sensitive, so feel free to use whatever format you find comfortable.
Entering the  HELP  command will print out the list of all possible commands.
Use the  [Up] / [Down] / [PageUp] / [PageDown]  keys to scroll through this window's contents.
Use the  [Left] / [Right]  keys to cycle through previously-entered commands.

It is said that states never truly die, that they simply change into something else. And while this is true, it misses the fact that for many the story is what matters. It mirrors our own fears, of change and of inevitable death. While the tale of the fall of Rome may not perfectly capture historical reality it's still a story that's important because of the meaning we find in it, and how historical narratives can be used both to inspire and control.

Violence is a pillar of human history. We are fundamentally a tribal species, and the simplest and most direct way tribes communicate is with blood. Study the birth of any human society, going back hundreds of thousands of years, and you will always find violence. And just as civilizations are born in blood they also die in blood. In the end all empires must fall. For there to be life there must also be death.

And so the chapter ended on another small, ambitious Dark Age tribe. This was a harsh era, one in which there was no room for mistakes. Your dream and your ambitions are over. Starving and resentful, your people have overthrown you.
